rationale: In the discussion section, the authors explicitly invoke Lim (2010) and summarize its core mechanism: contestants psychologically encode many nonwinning outcomes and react to social comparison losses. They then use that mechanism to explain their empirical pattern across performance tiers in single-segment contests. This is more than a passing literature-list mention because it provides a concrete interpretive lens for their results. However, the paper does not directly test or extend Lim (2010)'s original model, so the role is application-oriented.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Lim (2010)'s social loss bias argument to interpret its finding that lower performers in single-segment contests do not increase effort even when incremental performance metrics are used. It transfers that behavioral explanation to its own contest context rather than reusing Lim's model or experimentally testing the same mechanism.
